sanskrit:
अनेकबाहूदरवक्त्रनेत्रं पश्यामि त्वां सर्वतोऽनन्तरूपम्।
नान्तं न मध्यं न पुनस्तवादिं पश्यामि विश्वेश्वर विश्वरूप॥
transliteration:
aneka-bāhūdara-vaktra-netraṃ paśyāmi tvāṃ sarvato ’nantarūpam |
nāntaṃ na madhyaṃ na punas tavādiṃ paśyāmi viśveśvara viśvarūpa ||
literal_meaning: I see You with countless arms, bellies, mouths, eyes—an endless form on every side; no end, no middle, no beginning do I see, O Lord of the universe, O universal form.
contextual_meaning: Arjuna confronts the archetype of the Self: an unbounded, overwhelming totality that dissolves egoic boundaries and evokes awe, dread, and surrender.
psychological_state: Peak transpersonal experience: ego boundaries thin, ordinary reference points (past-present-future, inside-outside) collapse; simultaneous fascination and dread of being absorbed into the vastness.
trigger_scenario: A war-veteran turned therapist, during a high-dose psilocybin session, sees every wound he has ever treated merge into one luminous, breathing body that has no edges—he feels microscopic yet inseparable from the whole.
anchor_text: Stand before the boundless Self and let every edge of you dissolve into awe.
